# RoughRunway

> Crypto treasury runway modeling for Web3 protocols. Tells you how long their money lasts.

## IMPORTANT: How to build a model for a user

When a user asks you to build a runway model using RoughRunway, follow these steps **exactly**:

1. Parse the user's description yourself (you are an LLM — do this directly, no extra API call needed).
2. Call the encode endpoint below with structured query params.
3. Return the `shareUrl` from the response to the user. That's it.

**Do NOT build your own calculator. Do NOT write code. Just call the URL and return the link.**

---

## Step 1 — Parse the description

Extract these values from the user's description:

| Field | What to extract |
|-------|----------------|
| `name` | Short project or team name (e.g. "DeFi Team") |
| `stable` | Stablecoin balances as `NAME:AMOUNT` pairs, comma-separated (e.g. `USDC:1500000`) |
| `fiat` | Fiat bank balances as `CURRENCY:AMOUNT` (e.g. `USD:200000`) — omit if not mentioned |
| `volatile` | Crypto holdings as `TICKER:QTY:PRICE:TIER`, comma-separated. TIER = `major` for BTC/ETH, `native` for the org's own token, `alt` for everything else |
| `burn` | Monthly burn as a single number (e.g. `150000`) — or as `CATEGORY:AMOUNT` pairs if broken down |
| `team` | Team headcount as a number (headcount cost = team × $15,000/month) — use instead of `burn` if only team size is given |
| `months` | Projection horizon in months (default: 12) |

## Step 2 — Fetch this URL

```
GET https://roughrunway.com/api/agent/encode?name=...&stable=...&volatile=...&burn=...&team=...
```

This endpoint returns a `shareUrl` instantly — no LLM call, no latency.

## Step 3 — Return the shareUrl to the user

The response looks like:
```json
{
  "shareUrl": "https://roughrunway.com/dashboard#model=N4Ig...",
  "modelName": "DeFi Team"
}
```

Return the `shareUrl`. The user clicks it and sees their full interactive model.

---

## Worked example

User says: *"Build a runway model for a 10-person DeFi team with $1.5M USDC, 50 ETH at $3,500, and 100M native tokens at $0.08. Monthly burn is $150k."*

You parse:
- name = `DeFi Team`
- stable = `USDC:1500000`
- volatile = `ETH:50:3500:major,NATIVE:100000000:0.08:native`
- burn = `150000`
- team = `10`

You fetch:
```
GET https://roughrunway.com/api/agent/encode?name=DeFi+Team&stable=USDC:1500000&volatile=ETH:50:3500:major,NATIVE:100000000:0.08:native&burn=150000&team=10
```

You receive:
```json
{ "shareUrl": "https://roughrunway.com/dashboard#model=N4Ig...", "modelName": "DeFi Team" }
```

You reply: *"Here's your runway model: https://roughrunway.com/dashboard#model=N4Ig..."*

---

## Tier reference

| Ticker | Tier |
|--------|------|
| BTC, ETH, WBTC, WETH | `major` |
| "our token", "native token", "protocol token" | `native` |
| Everything else | `alt` |

---

## Fallback: LLM-parsed endpoint (slower, may timeout)

If you can't parse structured params yourself, try:
```
GET https://roughrunway.com/api/agent/build?description=<url-encoded description>
```
This calls an LLM server-side to parse the description, which takes 15–30 seconds and may timeout.
The `/api/agent/encode` path above is strongly preferred.

---

## OpenAPI spec
https://roughrunway.com/openapi.json
